examples.title: Pseudocode: Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence
examples.description: Demonstrating the relationship between ML and AI
examples.code:
ALGORITHM play_chess
INPUT: chess_board
OUTPUT: move

BEGIN
 IF using_ml THEN
 model = train_neural_network(data)
 move = model.predict(chess_board)
 ELSE
 move = rule_based_system(chess_board)
 END IF
 RETURN move
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the difference between a rule-based approach and a machine learning approach to playing chess.

examples.title: Python: Machine Learning and Data Science
examples.description: Demonstrating the relationship between ML and Data Science
examples.code:
def data_science_pipeline(data):
 # Descriptive analytics
 insights = descriptive_analytics(data)
 # Predictive analytics
 predictions = ml_model.predict(data)
 return insights, predictions
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates the use of machine learning in a data science pipeline.

examples.title: Python: Machine Learning and Statistics
examples.description: Demonstrating the relationship between ML and Statistics
examples.code:
def linear_regression(data):
 # Statistical modeling
 coefficients = ordinary_least_squares(data)
 return coefficients
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates the use of statistical modeling in machine learning.

examples.title: Python: Machine Learning and Computer Science
examples.description: Demonstrating the relationship between ML and Computer Science
examples.code:
def distributed_computing(data):
 # Apache Spark
 spark_context = create_spark_context()
 data = spark_context.parallelize(data)
 return data
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates the use of distributed computing in machine learning.
